phase1.output: (Window with ListView showing items, TableView showing people, with add/remove controls)
phase1.explanation:
ListView and TableView display collections of data:

ListView:
• Displays a list of items
• setItems(ObservableList)
• getSelectionModel().selectedItemProperty()
• Can add/remove dynamically

ObservableList:
• List that notifies UI of changes
• FXCollections.observableArrayList()
• Works with ListView and TableView

TableView:
• Displays tabular data
• Columns defined with TableColumn
• CellValueFactory binds to model properties

TableColumn setup:
• new TableColumn<Person, String>("Name")
• setCellValueFactory(new PropertyValueFactory<>("propertyName"))

Model class requirements:
• Must have property getters (nameProperty(), ageProperty())
• Or use SimpleXXXProperty fields

Selection handling:
• getSelectionModel().selectedItemProperty()
• getSelectionModel().getSelectedItem()
• getSelectionModel().getSelectedIndex()

Dynamic updates:
• Changes to ObservableList update UI automatically
• Works with add(), remove(), setAll()

CellValueFactory options:
• PropertyValueFactory (uses property names)
• Lambda: (cellData) -> cellData.getValue().property()

Common patterns:
• CRUD operations with ListView/TableView
• Selection tracking
• Dynamic data updates

⚠️ Always use ObservableList for dynamic data!

phase2.errorMessage: (TableView appears with header but NO DATA and blank columns!)

phase3.prompt:
Write a JavaFX program with:
1. A ListView<String> of programming languages: "Java", "Python", "C++", "JavaScript"
2. A TextField and Button to add new languages
3. A Button to remove the selected language
4. A Label that shows the selected language when clicked

Use ObservableList for the ListView data.
phase3.openingDialogue: ListView and TableView — display your data! 📋
